[observation] [warning] Both implementation files are entry points, suggesting this zone exports rather than encapsulates

[observation] [info] Small zone (5 files) with dedicated tests indicates focused, testable integration logic
[pattern] [info] Integration zone is thin (5 files) and focused — good separation of concerns for external tool coupling
[relationship] [info] Rex tool wrapper isolates PRD protocol details from core agent logic
[anti-pattern] [warning] rex.ts tool directly imports Rex types without a stable contract — Rex schema changes will break hench without compile-time guarantees

<insights>

- Handles task completion logic and Rex PRD integration
- Lower cohesion (0.67) reflects its bridging nature between agent and external tools
- Completion module bridges agent decisions to Rex PRD state machine
- Completion module acts as anti-corruption layer between agent decisions and Rex PRD mutations
- Completion validation tests are duplicated across two files (completion.test.ts and completion-validation.test.ts) — unclear separation of concerns

</insights>
